rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Approval For Transfer Of Payment Rights
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Monday, May 19, 2025, line 1 PETITIONER MPS SERVICES, LLC'S Hearing On Petition For Approval For Transfer Of Payment Rights The hearing on petition for approval of transfer of structured settlement payment rights is continued on the court's own motion to June 16, 2025. Petitioner is ordered to supplement its petition with declarations or competent evidence concerning the factors set out in Insurance Code 10139.5(a), (b), and (c). Those are all factors that the court is required to make express findings about, required to consider, or required to receive as part of the petition or through oral testimony or documentary evidence at the hearing. The petition is silent on many of the factors, and as to others it does not include competent evidence to support them (e.g. the settlement beneficiary's employment status and reason for seeking a transfer, which are not supported in any declaration). The supplement to the petition and any accompanying declarations shall be filed at least a week in advance of the continued hearing date. If petitioner would like to maintain the hearing date of May 19, 2025 and present oral testimony or documentary evidence at the hearing to satisfy the mandatory factors set out above, petitioner may notify the court by contesting this tentative ruling. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: Y.P. VS. WELLS FARGO & COMPANY, ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Monday, May 19, 2025, line 5, DEFENDANT SENTINEL INSURANCE COMPANY, LTD. DEMURRER to 2ND Amended COMPLAINT (tentative ruling part 1, see part 2) Defendant Sentinel Insurance Company, Ltd.'s ("Sentinel") demurrer to the Second Amended Complaint ("SAC") is overruled. Sentinel argues that there is no coverage under the parties' Computer Fraud Provision and in any event, the policy excludes plaintiff's claims. Both arguments founder. Computer Fraud Provision The policy states: We will pay up to $5,000 in any one occurrence for physical loss of or physical damage to "money", "securities", and other property having intrinsic value resulting directly from computer fraud. Computer fraud means any act of stealing property following and directly related to the use of any computer to fraudulently cause a transfer of that property from inside your premises or from a banking institution or similar safe depository, to a person (other than a "messenger") outside those premises or to a place outside those premises. (SAC, pdf 559.) The SAC sufficiently alleges that the fraud was "directly related to the use of any computer." (SAC, pars. 17-26.) Defendant does not convince the court to depart from its prior reliance on American Tooling Center v. Travelers Casualty and Surety Co. (6th Cir. 2018) 895 F.3d 455. There, the court explained that a direct loss is one resulting from an immediate or proximate cause, as distinct from remote or incidental causes. (American Tooling, 895 F.3d at 460.) The court liberally construes the complaint per CCP 452 and cannot determine this factual issue in defendant's favor on demurrer. Defendant next contends that the False Pretense exclusion bars plaintiff's claims. The exclusion states: False Pretense: Voluntary parting with any property by you or anyone else to whom you have entrusted the property if induced to do so by any fraudulent scheme, trick, device, or false pretense. (SAC, pdf 524.) Defendant's argument fails. The False Pretense exclusion generally pertains to special property coverage. (SAC, pdf 522-524.) The Super Stretch for Law Offices coverage, which includes the Computer Fraud provision, modifies the special property coverage form and does not include the exclusion. (SAC, pdf. 558-563.) The Super Stretch for Law Offices coverage expressly states that "This endorsement modifies insurance provided under the following SPECIAL PROPERTY COVERAGE FORM." (SAC, pdf 558.) (end of tentative ruling part 1 see part 2) = (301/CVA)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Monday, May 19, 2025, line 7, 1 - PLAINTIFF MICHAEL THOMAS' Motion To Have The Truth Of Matters Requested For Admission To Seamus Collins Be Deemed Admitted And For Sanctions Prior to the hearing on Michael Thomas's motion for deemed-admission orders as to his requests for admissions served on Seamus Collins, defendant Collins served responses. The court continued the matter for hearing and now concludes that the responses were substantially compliant. The court denies the request for a deemed-admitted order. (See CCP 2033.280(c).) Sanctions in this circumstance are mandatory and the court orders Collins to remit sanctions of $2660 payable to Thomas within 20 days of entry of this order. rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM MCDONNELL VS. THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: UNSEALING DOCUMENTS CONDITIONALLY LODGED UNDER SEAL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Monday, May 19, 2025, line 9. TENTATIVE RULING POSTED LATE AT 3:33 P.M. ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: UNSEALING DOCUMENTS CONDITIONALLY LODGED UNDER SEAL. The City and Plaintiff William McDonnell have stipulated that a document filed in this court on February 20, 2025, titled "Sequestered Documents Re: Plaintiff William McDonnell's Notice of Motion and Motion Requesting In Camera Review and Judicial Determination," was not properly filed and should be stricken from the record because it contains privileged communications. The court hereby discharges its order to show cause and strikes the filing. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.)
